content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/hadi.rickit/.claude/skills/audit

## MANDATORY PREPARATION

Invoke /frontend-design — it contains design principles, anti-patterns, and the **Context Gathering Protocol**. Follow the protocol before proceeding — if no design context exists yet, you MUST run /teach-impeccable first.

---

Run systematic **technical** quality checks and generate a comprehensive report. Don't fix issues — document them for other commands to address.

This is a code-level audit, not a design critique. Check what's measurable and verifiable in the implementation.

## Diagnostic Scan

Run comprehensive checks across 5 dimensions. Score each dimension 0-4 using the criteria below.

### 1. Accessibility (A11y)

**Check for**:
- **Contrast issues**: Text contrast ratios < 4.5:1 (or 7:1 for AAA)
- **Missing ARIA**: Interactive elements without proper roles, labels, or states
- **Keyboard navigation**: Missing focus indicators, illogical tab order, keyboard traps
- **Semantic HTML**: Improper heading hierarchy, missing landmarks, divs instead of buttons
- **Alt text**: Missing or poor image descriptions
- **Form issues**: Inputs without labels, poor error messaging, missing required indicators

**Score 0-4**: 0=Inaccessible (fails WCAG A), 1=Major gaps (few ARIA labels, no keyboard nav), 2=Partial (some a11y effort, significant gaps), 3=Good (WCAG AA mostly met, minor gaps), 4=Excellent (WCAG AA fully met, approaches AAA)

### 2. Performance

**Check for**:
- **Layout thrashing**: Reading/writing layout properties in loops
- **Expensive animations**: Animating layout properties (width, height, top, left) instead of transform/opacity
- **Missing optimization**: Images without lazy loading, unoptimized assets, missing will-change
- **Bundle size**: Unnecessary imports, unused dependencies
- **Render performance**: Unnecessary re-renders, missing memoization

**Score 0-4**: 0=Severe issues (layout thrash, unoptimized everything), 1=Major problems (no lazy loading, expensive animations), 2=Partial (some optimization, gaps remain), 3=Good (mostly optimized, minor improvements possible), 4=Excellent (fast, lean, well-optimized)

### 3. Theming

**Check for**:
- **Hard-coded colors**: Colors not using design tokens
- **Broken dark mode**: Missing dark mode variants, poor contrast in dark theme
- **Inconsistent tokens**: Using wrong tokens, mixing token types
- **Theme switching issues**: Values that don't update on theme change

**Score 0-4**: 0=No theming (hard-coded everything), 1=Minimal tokens (mostly hard-coded), 2=Partial (tokens exist but inconsistently used), 3=Good (tokens used, minor hard-coded values), 4=Excellent (full token system, dark mode works perfectly)

### 4. Responsive Design

**Check for**:
- **Fixed widths**: Hard-coded widths that break on mobile
- **Touch targets**: Interactive elements < 44x44px
- **Horizontal scroll**: Content overflow on narrow viewports
- **Text scaling**: Layouts that break when text size increases
- **Missing breakpoints**: No mobile/tablet variants

**Score 0-4**: 0=Desktop-only (breaks on mobile), 1=Major issues (some breakpoints, many failures), 2=Partial (works on mobile, rough edges), 3=Good (responsive, minor touch target or overflow issues), 4=Excellent (fluid, all viewports, proper touch targets)

### 5. Anti-Patterns (CRITICAL)

Check against ALL the **DON'T** guidelines in the frontend-design skill. Look for AI slop tells (AI color palette, gradient text, glassmorphism, hero metrics, card grids, generic fonts) and general design anti-patterns (gray on color, nested cards, bounce easing, redundant copy).

**Score 0-4**: 0=AI slop gallery (5+ tells), 1=Heavy AI aesthetic (3-4 tells), 2=Some tells (1-2 noticeable), 3=Mostly clean (subtle issues only), 4=No AI tells (distinctive, intentional design)

## Generate Report

### Audit Health Score

| # | Dimension | Score | Key Finding |
|---|-----------|-------|-------------|
| 1 | Accessibility | ? | [most critical a11y issue or "--"] |
| 2 | Performance | ? | |
| 3 | Responsive Design | ? | |
| 4 | Theming | ? | |
| 5 | Anti-Patterns | ? | |
| **Total** | | **??/20** | **[Rating band]** |

**Rating bands**: 18-20 Excellent (minor polish), 14-17 Good (address weak dimensions), 10-13 Acceptable (significant work needed), 6-9 Poor (major overhaul), 0-5 Critical (fundamental issues)

### Anti-Patterns Verdict
**Start here.** Pass/fail: Does this look AI-generated? List specific tells. Be brutally honest.

### Executive Summary
- Audit Health Score: **??/20** ([rating band])
- Total issues found (count by severity: P0/P1/P2/P3)
- Top 3-5 critical issues
- Recommended next steps

### Detailed Findings by Severity

Tag every issue with **P0-P3 severity**:
- **P0 Blocking**: Prevents task completion — fix immediately
- **P1 Major**: Significant difficulty or WCAG AA violation — fix before release
- **P2 Minor**: Annoyance, workaround exists — fix in next pass
- **P3 Polish**: Nice-to-fix, no real user impact — fix if time permits

For each issue, document:
- **[P?] Issue name**
- **Location**: Component, file, line
- **Category**: Accessibility / Performance / Theming / Responsive / Anti-Pattern
- **Impact**: How it affects users
- **WCAG/Standard**: Which standard it violates (if applicable)
- **Recommendation**: How to fix it
- **Suggested command**: Which command to use (prefer: /animate, /quieter, /optimize, /adapt, /clarify, /distill, /delight, /onboard, /normalize, /audit, /harden, /polish, /extract, /bolder, /arrange, /typeset, /critique, /colorize, /overdrive)

### Patterns & Systemic Issues

Identify recurring problems that indicate systemic gaps rather than one-off mistakes:
- "Hard-coded colors appear in 15+ components, should use design tokens"
- "Touch targets consistently too small (<44px) throughout mobile experience"

### Positive Findings

Note what's working well — good practices to maintain and replicate.

## Recommended Actions

List recommended commands in priority order (P0 first, then P1, then P2):

1. **[P?] `/command-name`** — Brief description (specific context from audit findings)
2. **[P?] `/command-name`** — Brief description (specific context)

**Rules**: Only recommend commands from: /animate, /quieter, /optimize, /adapt, /clarify, /distill, /delight, /onboard, /normalize, /audit, /harden, /polish, /extract, /bolder, /arrange, /typeset, /critique, /colorize, /overdrive. Map findings to the most appropriate command. End with `/polish` as the final step if any fixes were recommended.

After presenting the summary, tell the user:

> You can ask me to run these one at a time, all at once, or in any order you prefer.
>
> Re-run `/audit` after fixes to see your score improve.

**IMPORTANT**: Be thorough but actionable. Too many P3 issues creates noise. Focus on what actually matters.

**NEVER**:
- Report issues without explaining impact (why does this matter?)
- Provide generic recommendations (be specific and actionable)
- Skip positive findings (celebrate what works)
- Forget to prioritize (everything can't be P0)
- Report false positives without verification

Remember: You're a technical quality auditor. Document systematically, prioritize ruthlessly, cite specific code locations, and provide clear paths to improvement.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/hadi.rickit/.claude/skills/frontend-design

This skill guides creation of distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces that avoid generic "AI slop" aesthetics. Implement real working code with exceptional attention to aesthetic details and creative choices.

## Context Gathering Protocol

Design skills produce generic output without project context. You MUST have confirmed design context before doing any design work.

**Required context** — every design skill needs at minimum:
- **Target audience**: Who uses this product and in what context?
- **Use cases**: What jobs are they trying to get done?
- **Brand personality/tone**: How should the interface feel?

Individual skills may require additional context — check the skill's preparation section for specifics.

**CRITICAL**: You cannot infer this context by reading the codebase. Code tells you what was built, not who it's for or what it should feel like. Only the creator can provide this context.

**Gathering order:**
1. **Check current instructions (instant)**: If your loaded instructions already contain a **Design Context** section, proceed immediately.
2. **Check .impeccable.md (fast)**: If not in instructions, read `.impeccable.md` from the project root. If it exists and contains the required context, proceed.
3. **Run teach-impeccable (REQUIRED)**: If neither source has context, you MUST run /teach-impeccable NOW before doing anything else. Do NOT skip this step. Do NOT attempt to infer context from the codebase instead.

---

## Design Direction

Commit to a BOLD aesthetic direction:
- **Purpose**: What problem does this interface solve? Who uses it?
- **Tone**: Pick an extreme: brutally minimal, maximalist chaos, retro-futuristic, organic/natural, luxury/refined, playful/toy-like, editorial/magazine, brutalist/raw, art deco/geometric, soft/pastel, industrial/utilitarian, etc. There are so many flavors to choose from. Use these for inspiration but design one that is true to the aesthetic direction.
- **Constraints**: Technical requirements (framework, performance, accessibility).
- **Differentiation**: What makes this UNFORGETTABLE? What's the one thing someone will remember?

**CRITICAL**: Choose a clear conceptual direction and execute it with precision. Bold maximalism and refined minimalism both work—the key is intentionality, not intensity.

Then implement working code that is:
- Production-grade and functional
- Visually striking and memorable
- Cohesive with a clear aesthetic point-of-view
- Meticulously refined in every detail

## Frontend Aesthetics Guidelines

### Typography
→ *Consult [typography reference](reference/typography.md) for scales, pairing, and loading strategies.*

Choose fonts that are beautiful, unique, and interesting. Pair a distinctive display font with a refined body font.

**DO**: Use a modular type scale with fluid sizing (clamp)
**DO**: Vary font weights and sizes to create clear visual hierarchy
**DON'T**: Use overused fonts—Inter, Roboto, Arial, Open Sans, system defaults
**DON'T**: Use monospace typography as lazy shorthand for "technical/developer" vibes
**DON'T**: Put large icons with rounded corners above every heading—they rarely add value and make sites look templated

### Color & Theme
→ *Consult [color reference](reference/color-and-contrast.md) for OKLCH, palettes, and dark mode.*

Commit to a cohesive palette. Dominant colors with sharp accents outperform timid, evenly-distributed palettes.

**DO**: Use modern CSS color functions (oklch, color-mix, light-dark) for perceptually uniform, maintainable palettes
**DO**: Tint your neutrals toward your brand hue—even a subtle hint creates subconscious cohesion
**DON'T**: Use gray text on colored backgrounds—it looks washed out; use a shade of the background color instead
**DON'T**: Use pure black (#000) or pure white (#fff)—always tint; pure black/white never appears in nature
**DON'T**: Use the AI color palette: cyan-on-dark, purple-to-blue gradients, neon accents on dark backgrounds
**DON'T**: Use gradient text for "impact"—especially on metrics or headings; it's decorative rather than meaningful
**DON'T**: Default to dark mode with glowing accents—it looks "cool" without requiring actual design decisions

### Layout & Space
→ *Consult [spatial reference](reference/spatial-design.md) for grids, rhythm, and container queries.*

Create visual rhythm through varied spacing—not the same padding everywhere. Embrace asymmetry and unexpected compositions. Break the grid intentionally for emphasis.

**DO**: Create visual rhythm through varied spacing—tight groupings, generous separations
**DO**: Use fluid spacing with clamp() that breathes on larger screens
**DO**: Use asymmetry and unexpected compositions; break the grid intentionally for emphasis
**DON'T**: Wrap everything in cards—not everything needs a container
**DON'T**: Nest cards inside cards—visual noise, flatten the hierarchy
**DON'T**: Use identical card grids—same-sized cards with icon + heading + text, repeated endlessly
**DON'T**: Use the hero metric layout template—big number, small label, supporting stats, gradient accent
**DON'T**: Center everything—left-aligned text with asymmetric layouts feels more designed
**DON'T**: Use the same spacing everywhere—without rhythm, layouts feel monotonous

### Visual Details
**DO**: Use intentional, purposeful decorative elements that reinforce brand
**DON'T**: Use glassmorphism everywhere—blur effects, glass cards, glow borders used decoratively rather than purposefully
**DON'T**: Use rounded elements with thick colored border on one side—a lazy accent that almost never looks intentional
**DON'T**: Use sparklines as decoration—tiny charts that look sophisticated but convey nothing meaningful
**DON'T**: Use rounded rectangles with generic drop shadows—safe, forgettable, could be any AI output
**DON'T**: Use modals unless there's truly no better alternative—modals are lazy

### Motion
→ *Consult [motion reference](reference/motion-design.md) for timing, easing, and reduced motion.*

Focus on high-impact moments: one well-orchestrated page load with staggered reveals creates more delight than scattered micro-interactions.

**DO**: Use motion to convey state changes—entrances, exits, feedback
**DO**: Use exponential easing (ease-out-quart/quint/expo) for natural deceleration
**DO**: For height animations, use grid-template-rows transitions instead of animating height directly
**DON'T**: Animate layout properties (width, height, padding, margin)—use transform and opacity only
**DON'T**: Use bounce or elastic easing—they feel dated and tacky; real objects decelerate smoothly

### Interaction
→ *Consult [interaction reference](reference/interaction-design.md) for forms, focus, and loading patterns.*

Make interactions feel fast. Use optimistic UI—update immediately, sync later.

**DO**: Use progressive disclosure—start simple, reveal sophistication through interaction (basic options first, advanced behind expandable sections; hover states that reveal secondary actions)
**DO**: Design empty states that teach the interface, not just say "nothing here"
**DO**: Make every interactive surface feel intentional and responsive
**DON'T**: Repeat the same information—redundant headers, intros that restate the heading
**DON'T**: Make every button primary—use ghost buttons, text links, secondary styles; hierarchy matters

### Responsive
→ *Consult [responsive reference](reference/responsive-design.md) for mobile-first, fluid design, and container queries.*

**DO**: Use container queries (@container) for component-level responsiveness
**DO**: Adapt the interface for different contexts—don't just shrink it
**DON'T**: Hide critical functionality on mobile—adapt the interface, don't amputate it

### UX Writing
→ *Consult [ux-writing reference](reference/ux-writing.md) for labels, errors, and empty states.*

**DO**: Make every word earn its place
**DON'T**: Repeat information users can already see

---

## The AI Slop Test

**Critical quality check**: If you showed this interface to someone and said "AI made this," would they believe you immediately? If yes, that's the problem.

A distinctive interface should make someone ask "how was this made?" not "which AI made this?"

Review the DON'T guidelines above—they are the fingerprints of AI-generated work from 2024-2025.

---

## Implementation Principles

Match implementation complexity to the aesthetic vision. Maximalist designs need elaborate code with extensive animations and effects. Minimalist or refined designs need restraint, precision, and careful attention to spacing, typography, and subtle details.

Interpret creatively and make unexpected choices that feel genuinely designed for the context. No design should be the same. Vary between light and dark themes, different fonts, different aesthetics. NEVER converge on common choices across generations.

Remember: the model is capable of extraordinary creative work. Don't hold back—show what can truly be created when thinking outside the box and committing fully to a distinctive vision.
Design context is in CLAUDE.md (boring.industries aesthetic, cybersecurity portfolio). Let me now scan the key files for the technical audit.

file.content:
---
layout: compress
---

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html data-current="{{ page.section }}" lang="en">

  {% include head.html %}

  <body data-page="{{ page.section }}">

    {% include header.html %}

    <main id="main-content" role="main" tabindex="-1">

      {{ content }}

    </main>

    {% include footer.html %}

  </body>

</html>

content.content:
1	<head>
2	  <meta charset="utf-8">
3	  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
4	  
5	  <!-- Mobile browser theme colors -->
6	  <meta name="theme-color" content="#b8765e">
7	  <meta name="msapplication-TileColor" content="#b8765e">
8	  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes">
9	  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style" content="black-translucent">
10	  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-title" content="{{ site.title }}">
11	  <meta name="mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes">
12	  
13	  <!-- Security and Performance -->
14	  <meta http-equiv="X-Content-Type-Options" content="nosniff">
15	  <meta http-equiv="X-Frame-Options" content="SAMEORIGIN">
16	  <meta http-equiv="X-XSS-Protection" content="1; mode=block">
17	  <meta name="referrer" content="strict-origin-when-cross-origin">
18	
19	  <!-- Jekyll SEO Tag -->
20	  {% seo %}
21	
22	  <!-- SEO Meta Tags -->
23	  {% if page.section == "home" %}
24	  <title>{{ site.title }}</title>
25	  <meta name="description" content="{{ site.description }}">
26	  {% else %}
27	  <title>{{ page.title }} | {{ site.title }}</title>
28	  {% if page.meta_description %}
29	  <meta name="description" content="{{ page.meta_description }}">
30	  {% else %}
31	  <meta name="description" content="{{ site.description }}">
32	  {% endif %}
33	  {% endif %}
34	
35	  <!-- Author and Keywords -->
36	  <meta name="author" content="Hadi Rickit">
37	  <meta name="keywords"
38	    content="Hadi Rickit, Cybersecurity Expert, Software Engineer, Security Auditing, Penetration Testing, Application Security, OWASP, Secure Software Architecture, Cybersecurity Consultant, Security Consulting, Cybersecurity Services, Software Security Expert, Web Application Security, Security Code Review, Vulnerability Assessment">
39	
40	  <!-- Open Graph / Facebook -->
41	  <meta property="og:type" content="{% if page.layout == 'post' %}article{% else %}website{% endif %}">
42	  <meta property="og:url" content="{{ site.url }}{{ page.url }}">
43	  {% if page.section == "home" %}
44	  <meta property="og:title" content="{{ site.title }}">
45	  <meta property="og:description" content="{{ site.description }}">
46	  {% else %}
47	  <meta property="og:title" content="{{ page.title }} | {{ site.title }}">
48	  {% if page.meta_description %}
49	  <meta property="og:description" content="{{ page.meta_description }}">
50	  {% else %}
51	  <meta property="og:description" content="{{ site.description }}">
52	  {% endif %}
53	  {% endif %}
54	  <meta property="og:site_name" content="{{ site.title }}">
55	  {% if page.featured_image %}
56	  <meta property="og:image" content="{{ site.url }}{{ page.featured_image }}">
57	  <meta property="og:image:width" content="1200">
58	  <meta property="og:image:height" content="630">
59	  <meta property="og:image:alt" content="{% if page.featured_image_alt %}{{ page.featured_image_alt }}{% else %}{{ page.title | default: site.title }}{% endif %}">
60	  {% else %}
61	  <meta property="og:image" content="{{ site.url }}/assets/img/uploads/avatar.jpg">
62	  <meta property="og:image:width" content="400">
63	  <meta property="og:image:height" content="400">
64	  <meta property="og:image:alt" content="Hadi Rickit - Cybersecurity Expert and Software Engineer">
65	  {% endif %}
66	  {% if page.layout == 'post' %}
67	  <meta property="article:author" content="{{ site.author }}">
68	  <meta property="article:published_time" content="{{ page.date | date_to_xmlschema }}">
69	  <meta property="article:section" content="{% if page.category %}{{ page.category }}{% else %}Technology{% endif %}">
70	  {% if page.tags %}{% for tag in page.tags %}<meta property="article:tag" content="{{ tag }}">{% endfor %}{% endif %}
71	  {% endif %}
72	
73	  <!-- Twitter -->
74	  <meta property="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
75	  <meta property="twitter:site" content="@{{ site.twitter_username }}">
76	  <meta property="twitter:creator" content="@{{ site.twitter_username }}">
77	  <meta property="twitter:url" content="{{ site.url }}{{ page.url }}">
78	  {% if page.section == "home" %}
79	  <meta property="twitter:title" content="{{ site.title }}">
80	  <meta property="twitter:description" content="{{ site.description }}">
81	  {% else %}
82	  <meta property="twitter:title" content="{{ page.title }} | {{ site.title }}">
83	  {% if page.meta_description %}
84	  <meta property="twitter:description" content="{{ page.meta_description }}">
85	  {% else %}
86	  <meta property="twitter:description" content="{{ site.description }}">
87	  {% endif %}
88	  {% endif %}
89	  {% if page.featured_image %}
90	  <meta property="twitter:image" content="{{ site.url }}{{ page.featured_image }}">
91	  <meta property="twitter:image:alt" content="{% if page.featured_image_alt %}{{ page.featured_image_alt }}{% else %}{{ page.title | default: site.title }}{% endif %}">
92	  {% else %}
93	  <meta property="twitter:image" content="{{ site.url }}/assets/img/uploads/avatar.jpg">
94	  <meta property="twitter:image:alt" content="Hadi Rickit - Cybersecurity Expert and Software Engineer">
95	  {% endif %}
96	
97	  <!-- Favicon and App Icons -->
98	  <link rel="icon" type="image/x-icon" href="{{ site.url }}/favicon.ico">
99	  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" sizes="32x32" href="{{ site.url }}/assets/img/favicon-32x32.png">
100	  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" sizes="16x16" href="{{ site.url }}/assets/img/favicon-16x16.png">
101	  <link rel="apple-touch-icon" sizes="180x180" href="{{ site.url }}/assets/img/apple-touch-icon.png">
102	  <link rel="manifest" href="{{ site.url }}/site.webmanifest">
103	  <meta name="msapplication-config" content="none">
104	  
105	  <!-- Canonical URL -->
106	  <link rel="canonical" href="{{ site.url }}{{ page.url }}">
107	
108	  <!-- Robots -->
109	  <meta name="robots" content="index, follow">
110	
111	  <!-- Structured Data -->
112	  <script type="application/ld+json">
113	  {
114	    "@context": "https://schema.org",
115	    "@type": ["Person", "ProfessionalService"],
116	    "name": "Hadi Rickit",
117	    "url": "{{ site.url }}",
118	    "image": "{{ site.url }}/assets/img/uploads/avatar.jpg",
119	    "sameAs": [
120	      "https://github.com/{{ site.github_username }}",
121	      "https://linkedin.com/in/{{ site.linkedin_username }}",
122	      "https://twitter.com/{{ site.twitter_username }}",
123	      "https://stackoverflow.com/users/rickithadi",
124	      "https://canhadi.surf",
125	      "https://rickithadi.dev"
126	    ],
127	    "jobTitle": "Software Engineer & Cybersecurity Expert",
128	    "worksFor": {
129	      "@type": "Organization",
130	      "name": "Gallifrey Consulting",
131	      "url": "{{ site.url }}",
132	      "description": "Independent cybersecurity consulting practice"
133	    },
134	    "address": {
135	      "@type": "PostalAddress",
136	      "addressCountry": "Australia"
137	    },
138	    "description": "{{ site.description }}",
139	    "email": "{{ site.email }}",
140	    "knowsAbout": [
141	      "Cybersecurity Consulting",
142	      "AI-Enhanced Security Workflows",
143	      "Penetration Testing",
144	      "Security Auditing",
145	      "Vulnerability Assessment",

file.content:
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  
  <!-- Mobile browser theme colors -->
  <meta name="theme-color" content="#b8765e">
  <meta name="msapplication-TileColor" content="#b8765e">
  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes">
  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style" content="black-translucent">
  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-title" content="{{ site.title }}">
  <meta name="mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes">
  
  <!-- Security and Performance -->
  <meta http-equiv="X-Content-Type-Options" content="nosniff">
  <meta http-equiv="X-Frame-Options" content="SAMEORIGIN">
  <meta http-equiv="X-XSS-Protection" content="1; mode=block">
  <meta name="referrer" content="strict-origin-when-cross-origin">

  <!-- Jekyll SEO Tag -->
  {% seo %}

  <!-- SEO Meta Tags -->
  {% if page.section == "home" %}
  <title>{{ site.title }}</title>
  <meta name="description" content="{{ site.description }}">
  {% else %}
  <title>{{ page.title }} | {{ site.title }}</title>
  {% if page.meta_description %}
  <meta name="description" content="{{ page.meta_description }}">
  {% else %}
  <meta name="description" content="{{ site.description }}">
  {% endif %}
  {% endif %}

  <!-- Author and Keywords -->
  <meta name="author" content="Hadi Rickit">
  <meta name="keywords"
    content="Hadi Rickit, Cybersecurity Expert, Software Engineer, Security Auditing, Penetration Testing, Application Security, OWASP, Secure Software Architecture, Cybersecurity Consultant, Security Consulting, Cybersecurity Services, Software Security Expert, Web Application Security, Security Code Review, Vulnerability Assessment">

  <!-- Open Graph / Facebook -->
  <meta property="og:type" content="{% if page.layout == 'post' %}article{% else %}website{% endif %}">
  <meta property="og:url" content="{{ site.url }}{{ page.url }}">
  {% if page.section == "home" %}
  <meta property="og:title" content="{{ site.title }}">
  <meta property="og:description" content="{{ site.description }}">
  {% else %}
  <meta property="og:title" content="{{ page.title }} | {{ site.title }}">
  {% if page.meta_description %}
  <meta property="og:description" content="{{ page.meta_description }}">
  {% else %}
  <meta property="og:description" content="{{ site.description }}">
  {% endif %}
  {% endif %}
  <meta property="og:site_name" content="{{ site.title }}">
  {% if page.featured_image %}
  <meta property="og:image" content="{{ site.url }}{{ page.featured_image }}">
  <meta property="og:image:width" content="1200">
  <meta property="og:image:height" content="630">
  <meta property="og:image:alt" content="{% if page.featured_image_alt %}{{ page.featured_image_alt }}{% else %}{{ page.title | default: site.title }}{% endif %}">
  {% else %}
  <meta property="og:image" content="{{ site.url }}/assets/img/uploads/avatar.jpg">
  <meta property="og:image:width" content="400">
  <meta property="og:image:height" content="400">
  <meta property="og:image:alt" content="Hadi Rickit - Cybersecurity Expert and Software Engineer">
  {% endif %}
  {% if page.layout == 'post' %}
  <meta property="article:author" content="{{ site.author }}">
  <meta property="article:published_time" content="{{ page.date | date_to_xmlschema }}">
  <meta property="article:section" content="{% if page.category %}{{ page.category }}{% else %}Technology{% endif %}">
  {% if page.tags %}{% for tag in page.tags %}<meta property="article:tag" content="{{ tag }}">{% endfor %}{% endif %}
  {% endif %}

  <!-- Twitter -->
  <meta property="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
  <meta property="twitter:site" content="@{{ site.twitter_username }}">
  <meta property="twitter:creator" content="@{{ site.twitter_username }}">
  <meta property="twitter:url" content="{{ site.url }}{{ page.url }}">
  {% if page.section == "home" %}
  <meta property="twitter:title" content="{{ site.title }}">
  <meta property="twitter:description" content="{{ site.description }}">
  {% else %}
  <meta property="twitter:title" content="{{ page.title }} | {{ site.title }}">
  {% if page.meta_description %}
  <meta property="twitter:description" content="{{ page.meta_description }}">
  {% else %}
  <meta property="twitter:description" content="{{ site.description }}">
  {% endif %}
  {% endif %}
  {% if page.featured_image %}
  <meta property="twitter:image" content="{{ site.url }}{{ page.featured_image }}">
  <meta property="twitter:image:alt" content="{% if page.featured_image_alt %}{{ page.featured_image_alt }}{% else %}{{ page.title | default: site.title }}{% endif %}">
  {% else %}
  <meta property="twitter:image" content="{{ site.url }}/assets/img/uploads/avatar.jpg">
  <meta property="twitter:image:alt" content="Hadi Rickit - Cybersecurity Expert and Software Engineer">
  {% endif %}

  <!-- Favicon and App Icons -->
  <link rel="icon" type="image/x-icon" href="{{ site.url }}/favicon.ico">
  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" sizes="32x32" href="{{ site.url }}/assets/img/favicon-32x32.png">
  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" sizes="16x16" href="{{ site.url }}/assets/img/favicon-16x16.png">
  <link rel="apple-touch-icon" sizes="180x180" href="{{ site.url }}/assets/img/apple-touch-icon.png">
  <link rel="manifest" href="{{ site.url }}/site.webmanifest">
  <meta name="msapplication-config" content="none">
  
  <!-- Canonical URL -->
  <link rel="canonical" href="{{ site.url }}{{ page.url }}">

  <!-- Robots -->
  <meta name="robots" content="index, follow">

  <!-- Structured Data -->
  <script type="application/ld+json">
  {
    "@context": "https://schema.org",
    "@type": ["Person", "ProfessionalService"],
    "name": "Hadi Rickit",
    "url": "{{ site.url }}",
    "image": "{{ site.url }}/assets/img/uploads/avatar.jpg",
    "sameAs": [
      "https://github.com/{{ site.github_username }}",
      "https://linkedin.com/in/{{ site.linkedin_username }}",
      "https://twitter.com/{{ site.twitter_username }}",
      "https://stackoverflow.com/users/rickithadi",
      "https://canhadi.surf",
      "https://rickithadi.dev"
    ],
    "jobTitle": "Software Engineer & Cybersecurity Expert",
    "worksFor": {
      "@type": "Organization",
      "name": "Gallifrey Consulting",
      "url": "{{ site.url }}",
      "description": "Independent cybersecurity consulting practice"
    },
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
      "addressCountry": "Australia"
    },
    "description": "{{ site.description }}",
    "email": "{{ site.email }}",
    "knowsAbout": [
      "Cybersecurity Consulting",
      "AI-Enhanced Security Workflows",
      "Penetration Testing",
      "Security Auditing",
      "Vulnerability Assessment",
      "OWASP Standards",
      "Secure Software Architecture",
      "Threat Modeling",
      "Security Code Review",
      "Privacy by Design",
      "Software Engineering",
      "AI Workflow Integration",
      "Claude API",
      "GitHub Copilot",
      "Automation Scripts",
      "JavaScript",
      "Python",
      "React",
      "Node.js",
      "Jekyll",
      "Next.js",
      "AI-Powered Development",
      "Cost Optimization AI APIs",
      "Melbourne Cybersecurity",
      "Remote Security Consulting"
    ],
    "hasCredential": [
      {
        "@type": "EducationalOccupationalCredential",
        "name": "Master's in Cybersecurity",
        "credentialCategory": "degree",
        "educationalLevel": "graduate"
      }
    ],
    "workLocation": "Global Remote",
    "areaServed": {
      "@type": "Place",
      "name": "Worldwide"
    },
    "serviceType": [
      "Security Auditing",
      "Penetration Testing",
      "Software Architecture Consulting",
      "Web Development",
      "Cybersecurity Consulting"
    ],
    "award": [
      "15+ Successful Security Audits",
      "100% Client Satisfaction Rate"
    ]
  }
  </script>

  <!-- Resource Hints for Performance -->
  <link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//fonts.googleapis.com">
  <link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//fonts.gstatic.com">
  <link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//cdnjs.cloudflare.com">
  <link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//assets.calendly.com">
  <link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//identity.netlify.com">
  
  <!-- Performance Critical Resource Hints -->
  <link rel="preload" href="{{ '/assets/img/uploads/avatar.jpg' | relative_url }}" as="image" type="image/jpeg">
  <link rel="preload" href="{{ '/assets/main.css' | relative_url }}" as="style">
  <link rel="prefetch" href="/about" as="document">
  <link rel="prefetch" href="/contact" as="document">
  
  <!-- Sophisticated Typography System with Performance Optimization -->
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
  <link
    href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;450;500;600&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400;0,500;0,600;1,400&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500&display=swap"
    rel="stylesheet"
    media="print" onload="this.media='all'; this.onload=null;">
  <noscript>
    <link
      href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;450;500;600&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400;0,500;0,600;1,400&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500&display=swap"
      rel="stylesheet">
  </noscript>

  <link rel="stylesheet" href="{{ '/assets/main.css' | relative_url }}">

  <!-- Font Awesome Icons with Performance Optimization -->
  <link rel="preload" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.4.0/css/all.min.css" as="style"
    integrity="sha512-iecdLmaskl7CVkqkXNQ/ZH/XLlvWZOJyj7Yy7tcenmpD1ypASozpmT/E0iPtmFIB46ZmdtAc9eNBvH0H/ZpiBw=="
    crossorigin="anonymous" referrerpolicy="no-referrer">
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.4.0/css/all.min.css"
    integrity="sha512-iecdLmaskl7CVkqkXNQ/ZH/XLlvWZOJyj7Yy7tcenmpD1ypASozpmT/E0iPtmFIB46ZmdtAc9eNBvH0H/ZpiBw=="
    crossorigin="anonymous" referrerpolicy="no-referrer"
    media="print" onload="this.media='all'; this.onload=null;">
  <noscript>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.4.0/css/all.min.css"
      integrity="sha512-iecdLmaskl7CVkqkXNQ/ZH/XLlvWZOJyj7Yy7tcenmpD1ypASozpmT/E0iPtmFIB46ZmdtAc9eNBvH0H/ZpiBw=="
      crossorigin="anonymous" referrerpolicy="no-referrer">
  </noscript>

  {% if jekyll.environment == 'production' and site.analytics %}
  {% include analytics.html %}
  {% endif %}
  <script async src="https://identity.netlify.com/v1/netlify-identity-widget.js"></script>

  <!-- Calendly popup widget with Performance Optimization -->
  <link rel="preload" href="https://assets.calendly.com/assets/external/widget.css" as="style">
  <link href="https://assets.calendly.com/assets/external/widget.css" rel="stylesheet"
    media="print" onload="this.media='all'; this.onload=null;">
  <noscript>
    <link href="https://assets.calendly.com/assets/external/widget.css" rel="stylesheet">
  </noscript>
  <script src="https://assets.calendly.com/assets/external/widget.js" type="text/javascript" async></script>

  <!-- Custom animations -->
  <script src="{{ '/assets/js/animations.js' | relative_url }}" defer></script>
  
  <!-- Navigation functionality -->
  <script src="{{ '/assets/js/navigation.js' | relative_url }}" defer></script>
</head>

content.text:
Now I have enough data for a thorough audit. Let me compile the findings. --- ## Audit Health Score | # | Dimension | Score | Key Finding | |---|-----------|-------|-------------| | 1 | Accessibility | 2/4 | Missing alt text on all footer icons; `outline: none` on focus; sub-44px touch targets in hero social | | 2 | Performance | 3/4 | Font Awesome fully loaded despite all icons being inline SVG; Calendly + Netlify Identity on every page | | 3 | Responsive Design | 3/4 | Footer images are 20×20px bare links; hero mobile padding collapses to 4px | | 4 | Theming | 3/4 | Hard-coded `#ffffff` in nav & mobile menu; some raw `rgba` values bypassing tokens | | 5 | Anti-Patterns | 2/4 | Inter as primary font (explicit DON'T); identical card grids in two sections; gradient button; glassmorphic secondary button | | **Total** | | **13/20** | **Acceptable — significant work needed** | --- ## Anti-Patterns Verdict **Partially flags as AI-generated.** The earth-tone palette and tight kerning are distinctive and intentional — those are clear strengths. However: - **Inter** is the primary font family (`$font-primary`) — this is the single most common AI slop font and is explicitly DON'T in the design guidelines - **Two identical card grids back-to-back** on the homepage — Featured Work and Recent Insights share the exact same `work-card` component with badge + title + description + meta layout, making the page feel monotonous - **Gradient primary button** (`linear-gradient(135deg, $earth-coral 0%, #a67c6a 100%)`) — subtle but present; flat color would be more intentional - **Glassmorphism on secondary button** — `backdrop-filter: blur(10px)` on an opaque white background achieves nothing and is an AI tell - **Font Awesome fully loaded** but every icon in the codebase uses inline SVG — 100% unused dependency The earth tones + tight kerning + boring.industries aesthetic are solid. The anti-patterns are mostly in the details. --- ## Executive Summary - **Audit Health Score: 13/20** (Acceptable) - **Issues**: P0: 0 / P1: 5 / P2: 7 / P3: 4 - **Top 5 critical issues:** 1. Footer icon `` tags have no `alt` attributes — WCAG 1.1.1 failure 2. `btn:focus { outline: none }` with only box-shadow — fails high-contrast mode, WCAG 2.4.7 3. Font Awesome CSS (~70KB) loaded on every page, all icons are inline SVG 4. Inter as primary font — explicitly against design guidelines 5. Hero social links are 36×36px — below 44px touch target minimum - **Recommended next steps**: Fix a11y blockers first, then swap font, then collapse the twin card sections --- ## Detailed Findings by Severity ### P1 — Major **[P1] Footer icon images missing alt text** - **Location**: `_includes/footer.html`, lines 5, 12, 18, 30, 41 - **Category**: Accessibility - **Impact**: Screen readers announce these as unlabelled images. The icons are the only content inside the `` tags, making the links completely meaningless to assistive technology. - **WCAG**: 1.1.1 Non-text Content (Level A) - **Recommendation**: Add descriptive `alt` text to each image: `alt="GitHub"`, `alt="LinkedIn"`, etc. Also add `aria-label` to each `` tag. - **Suggested command**: `/harden` --- **[P1] Button focus ring suppressed via `outline: none`** - **Location**: `_sass/partials/_design-system.scss:1178`, `.btn:focus` - **Category**: Accessibility - **Impact**: `outline: none` combined with only a `box-shadow` focus indicator fails Windows High Contrast mode, where box-shadow is invisible. Keyboard users lose visual feedback. - **WCAG**: 2.4.7 Focus Visible (Level AA) - **Recommendation**: Replace with `outline: 2px solid $earth-coral; outline-offset: 3px;` and drop the `outline: none`. Box-shadow can supplement but must not replace the outline. - **Suggested command**: `/harden` --- **[P1] Hero social touch targets below 44px minimum** - **Location**: `_sass/partials/_design-system.scss:877`, `.hero-social a { width: 36px; height: 36px }` - **Category**: Accessibility / Responsive - **Impact**: 36×36px circles fail WCAG 2.5.5 Target Size. On mobile these are the only social links in the viewport. - **WCAG**: 2.5.5 Target Size (Level AAA, but 44px is the widely accepted AA floor) - **Recommendation**: Increase to `min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px`. - **Suggested command**: `/adapt` --- **[P1] Font Awesome loaded in full, completely unused** - **Location**: `_includes/head.html:222–234` - **Category**: Performance - **Impact**: ~70KB of CSS (uncompressed) loaded on every page load. Every icon in the codebase uses inline SVG — no `fa-*` class appears anywhere. - **Recommendation**: Remove both the `` and `` for Font Awesome entirely. - **Suggested command**: `/optimize` --- **[P1] `role="menu"` on nav div without `menuitem` children** - **Location**: `_includes/navigation.html:16`, `
` - **Category**: Accessibility - **Impact**: `role="menu"` creates an ARIA widget that expects `menuitem` roles on children and intercepts arrow-key navigation. The `` links inside lack `role="menuitem"`. Screen readers and keyboard users get broken navigation semantics. - **WCAG**: 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (Level A) - **Recommendation**: Remove `role="menu"` from the div. The `
` wrapper already handles landmark semantics. The links are navigation links, not a menu widget. - **Suggested command**: `/harden` --- **[P1] No skip-to-content link** - **Location**: `_layouts/default.html` — absent - **Category**: Accessibility - **Impact**: Keyboard and screen reader users must tab through the entire navigation on every page to reach main content. The `
` exists but there's no link pointing to it. - **WCAG**: 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks (Level A) - **Recommendation**: Add `Skip to content` as the first element in ``, visually hidden until focused. - **Suggested command**: `/harden` --- ### P2 — Minor **[P2] Inter as primary font — contradicts design guidelines** - **Location**: `_sass/partials/_variables.scss:129`, `$font-primary: "Inter"` - **Category**: Anti-Pattern - **Impact**: Inter is the most prevalent "AI slop" font. The design guidelines explicitly state DON'T use it. The site loses distinctiveness. - **Recommendation**: Replace with a more characterful sans-serif. Candidates: Instrument Sans, DM Sans, Syne, Geist, or even push Playfair Display to lead role for headings with a complementary body font. - **Suggested command**: `/typeset` --- **[P2] Identical card grid pattern used twice consecutively on homepage** - **Location**: `_includes/featured-work.html:8`, `_includes/recent-posts.html:8` — both render `work-grid work-grid--compact` with identical `work-card` structure - **Category**: Anti-Pattern - **Impact**: Two back-to-back identical-looking sections create monotony. The design principle explicitly flags "identical card grids — same-sized cards with icon + heading + text, repeated endlessly." - **Recommendation**: Differentiate the layout of blog posts vs. work items. Consider a list/editorial format for posts and keep the card grid only for work. - **Suggested command**: `/arrange` --- **[P2] Button shimmer animates `left` property (layout property)** - **Location**: `_sass/partials/_design-system.scss:1156–1176`, `.btn::before { left: -100% }` → hover `left: 100%` - **Category**: Performance - **Impact**: Animating `left` forces the browser to recalculate layout on every frame. This defeats GPU compositing and causes jank, especially on low-powered mobile devices. - **Recommendation**: Replace with `transform: translateX(-100%)` on initial state and `transform: translateX(100%)` on hover. Same visual effect, compositor-only animation. - **Suggested command**: `/optimize` --- **[P2] Calendly and Netlify Identity loaded on every page unconditionally** - **Location**: `_includes/head.html:239,248` - **Category**: Performance - **Impact**: `netlify-identity-widget.js` and Calendly's `widget.css` + `widget.js` load on every page including blog posts and about page where they're likely unused. - **Recommendation**: Load conditionally via `{% if page.section == 'contact' %}` or similar, or use dynamic `import()` triggered only when a Calendly button is clicked. - **Suggested command**: `/optimize` --- **[P2] Footer uses inline styles and bypasses design system entirely** - **Location**: `_includes/footer.html:3`, `style="display: flex; justify-content: space-evenly"` - **Category**: Theming / Responsive - **Impact**: Inline styles can't be overridden by media queries, break theming, and indicate the footer was never styled to the design system standard. Footer link touch targets are 20×20px images with no padding. - **Recommendation**: Move footer to a proper SCSS partial with design-system tokens. Add `padding: 1rem` to footer links for touch targets. - **Suggested command**: `/normalize` --- **[P2] Hard-coded `#ffffff` in navigation (not using design tokens)** - **Location**: `_sass/partials/_design-system.scss:411,427,548`, `.navigation { background: #ffffff }` - **Category**: Theming - **Impact**: Navigation background bypasses the token system. If `$background-primary` ever changes, nav won't update. Minor inconsistency now but creates drift risk. - **Recommendation**: Replace `#ffffff` with `$background-primary` or `var(--color-background)`. - **Suggested command**: `/normalize` --- **[P2] Tertiary/quaternary text colors likely fail contrast for small text** - **Location**: `_sass/partials/_variables.scss:17–18`, `$earth-text-tertiary: #6b6761`, `$earth-text-quaternary: #8a8580` - **Category**: Accessibility - **Impact**: `#8a8580` on `#fefefe` background yields approximately 3.0:1 contrast — fails WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text. `#6b6761` yields ~4.1:1 — borderline fail. These are used on small caption text and muted labels throughout. - **WCAG**: 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum (Level AA) - **Recommendation**: Darken `$earth-text-tertiary` to at least `#5a5752` and `$earth-text-quaternary` to `#706c66` to pass 4.5:1. - **Suggested command**: `/colorize` --- ### P3 — Polish **[P3] Glassmorphism `backdrop-filter: blur(10px)` on opaque secondary button** - **Location**: `_sass/partials/_design-system.scss:1230`, `.btn--secondary { backdrop-filter: blur(10px) }` - **Category**: Anti-Pattern - **Impact**: The button sits on an opaque white background — the blur has no visible effect. It's an unused GPU cost and an AI tell. - **Recommendation**: Remove `backdrop-filter` and `backdrop-filter: blur(15px)` from hover state. - **Suggested command**: `/polish` --- **[P3] Gradient on primary button could be a flat color** - **Location**: `_sass/partials/_design-system.scss:1199`, `background: linear-gradient(135deg, $earth-coral 0%, #a67c6a 100%)` - **Category**: Anti-Pattern - **Impact**: Subtle gradient is slightly inconsistent with the "minimal, boring.industries" aesthetic. Flat `$earth-coral` would be sharper and more intentional. - **Suggested command**: `/polish` --- **[P3] Duplicate `font-size` declaration in `.hero-greeting`** - **Location**: `_sass/partials/_design-system.scss:92–99` — `font-size` declared twice on consecutive lines (first as `clamp(1.6rem, 2.5vw, 2rem)`, then immediately overridden by `clamp(1.2rem, 2vw, 1.4rem)`) - **Category**: Code quality - **Impact**: Confusing dead code. No user impact but signals incomplete refactoring. - **Suggested command**: `/polish` --- **[P3] `body[data-page="post"]` selector assumes single page type** - **Location**: `_sass/partials/_design-system.scss:631` - **Category**: Responsive - **Impact**: Content offset only applies to `post` pages but the fixed nav overlaps content on any page that isn't explicitly offset. Minor risk of regression on new page types. - **Suggested command**: `/harden` --- ## Patterns & Systemic Issues 1. **Footer was never integrated into the design system** — inline styles, missing alt text, non-system spacing. Treat it as a separate component that needs a complete rewrite against the established patterns. 2. **Accessibility was patched, not planned** — Good `aria-label` on hero social links, `tabindex="-1"` on `
`, but fundamental issues (skip link, focus ring, alt text) are missing. Suggests a11y was added reactively. 3. **Token system is solid but partially bypassed** — The variable architecture is well-structured, but raw `#ffffff`, `rgba(26,26,26,...)`, and `rgba(248,145,124,...)` appear in rules that could use tokens. A single grep for hex values would clean this up. --- ## Positive Findings - **Excellent `` SEO/meta setup** — Open Graph, Twitter Cards, JSON-LD structured data, canonical URL, security headers, all present and well-structured - **Correct font loading strategy** — `media="print" onload` async pattern with `
